title: Portfolio Development
description: Designed and built personal web portfolio with interactive WebGL radar graphics and admin controls.

title: Google Cloud Agentic AI Day
description: Submitted conceptual proposal for LLM orchestration using Google Cloud infrastructure.

title: Egg Incubator Circuit Design
description: Wired full control circuit on stripboard with Arduino Uno, DHT22 sensor, and dual relay module.

title: IEEE Brain Battle Round 2
description: Competed in logical reasoning, technical MCQs, and live coding puzzles in IEEE community event.

title: Day 01: Introduction to IoT & Ecosystem
description: Inauguration of the PM-VIKAS IoT assistant training program. Introduced to IoT nodes, device-to-cloud architectures, real-world smart systems (cities, grid networks), and communication layers.

title: Day 02: Microcontroller Architectures & IDE
description: Study of Arduino Uno and ESP32 board layouts. Installation of Arduino IDE software, configuring port selections, writing basic C script routines, and flashing standard status LED programs.

title: Day 03: Basic Electronic Components
description: Detailed analysis of electronic devices including Resistors, Capacitors, and Light Emitting Diodes (LEDs). Practical hands-on breadboard wiring configurations and simple circuit design rules.

title: Day 04: Hardware Measurement & GPIOs
description: Configuring and testing digital/analog General Purpose Input/Output (GPIO) pins. Measuring voltage, loop currents, and resistance values across wired test circuits using a digital multimeter.

title: Day 05: Interfacing LDR (Light Sensors)
description: Interfacing Light Dependent Resistors (LDR) to read changing analog light intensity levels. Programmed thresholds to trigger output alerts based on ambient illumination changes.

title: Day 06: Interfacing DHT11 Temperature Sensor
description: Interfacing the DHT11 sensor to monitor environmental temperature and humidity levels. Written data validation logic to filter out noise fluctuations and format values for transmission.

title: Day 07: ESP32 WiFi & Network Protocols
description: Setting up the ESP32 microcontroller's Wi-Fi module. Establishing station (STA) connections to local networks and checking communication protocols (IP addressing, TCP client/server models).

title: Day 08: Blynk IoT Cloud Setup & Auth
description: Created developer accounts on Blynk IoT Cloud. Set up device templates, virtual datastreams, and generated secure Authentication Tokens. Configured basic mobile application dashboards.

title: Day 09: Real-time Cloud Telemetry (Blynk)
description: Programmed the ESP32 to upload environmental parameters (LDR intensity and DHT11 values) to the Blynk Cloud dashboard in real-time. Verified low-latency response and remote app widgets.

title: Day 10: ThingSpeak Logger & Capstone
description: Linked the ESP32 to the ThingSpeak server for long-term data logging and visualization. Assembled the final Capstone Project: an integrated Smart Home System that tracks sensors and controls output relays.

title: Day 11: Absent
description: Absent from the PM-VIKAS IoT training session.

title: Day 12: Absent
description: Absent from the PM-VIKAS IoT training session.

title: Day 13: Networking Fundamentals & Cisco Packet Tracer
description: Explored the fundamentals of computer networking, including networking, internetworking, the Network Layer, IPv4, IPv6, IP addressing, and subnet masking concepts. Gained hands-on experience in Cisco Packet Tracer by establishing a wired connection between two end devices using a Copper Cross-Over cable, configuring basic network settings, and verifying communication. Learned about different network cable types and their applications in wired communication. Additionally, collaborated with the team to explore MSME Hackathon 6.0 problem statements and brainstorm innovative IoT-based project ideas.

title: Day 14: DHCP Configuration & LAN Simulation in Cisco Packet Tracer
description: Configured a Local Area Network (LAN) in Cisco Packet Tracer by connecting a server, switch, hubs, and multiple end devices. Implemented DHCP to dynamically assign IP addresses while configuring the server with a static IP address. Verified network connectivity through ping tests between different hubs and the server, gaining hands-on experience in IP addressing, subnet masking, dynamic host configuration, and LAN communication.

title: Day 15: Router Configuration & Inter-Network Communication
description: Reviewed the previous day's DHCP-based LAN implementation and advanced to router configuration, exploring FastEthernet and GigabitEthernet interfaces for inter-network communication. Worked on implementing networks with both dynamic (DHCP) and static IP addressing by connecting an additional hub through a router and verifying communication between different subnets. Additionally, completed topology design tasks involving a DHCP-enabled LAN integrated with a static-IP network and a multi-router network consisting of five routers, five switches, and end devices to strengthen practical understanding of routing, IP addressing, and network connectivity in Cisco Packet Tracer.

title: Day 16: IoT Network Integration using Cisco Packet Tracer
description: Focused on IoT network integration using Cisco Packet Tracer. Configured routers using static routing to enable communication between different LANs and successfully connected a remote PC to an IoT server. Performed end-to-end connectivity testing using ping, identified and resolved routing and default gateway issues, and verified remote monitoring and control of IoT devices through the IoT Monitor interface.

title: Day 17: MSME Hackathon 6.0 – Idea Submission
description: Dedicated the day to researching innovative and cost-effective solutions for MSME Hackathon 6.0. Explored various problem statements, conducted prior-art and patent searches using the WIPO PatentScope database, and finalized the project idea: TeaPulse – Acoustic & Current-Signature Predictive Maintenance and Energy Optimization System for MSMEs.

title: Day 20: Cloud Computing & Go Services
description: Covered fundamentals of Cloud Computing including IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS service models with their features and real-world applications. Created and activated an AWS Cloud account with $100 cloud credits for practical learning. Installed and configured the Go (Golang) programming language, then developed and executed multiple Go-based HTTP web services: Hello World, Calculator, Greeting, Square Number, Temperature Converter, Weather Prediction, and other simple services. Tested all services locally through localhost, gaining hands-on experience in both cloud computing concepts and Go web service development.

title: Day 21: Docker & Virtualization
description: Learned the fundamentals of Docker and virtualization, including Docker Images, Containers, Dockerfiles, and the key differences between Virtual Machines and Docker containers. Practiced essential Docker commands for building, running, managing, and monitoring containers. Created a VMware account to explore virtualization technologies and a Tinkercad account for simulation-based learning. Gained a clear understanding of containerization, virtualization, and their role in modern application deployment and cloud environments.

title: Day 22: Absent due to Placement Drive
description: Absent from the PM-VIKAS IoT training session due to participation in the placement drive.

title: Day 23: AWS EC2, Docker Deployment & Industry Institution Interactive Meet (IIIM)
description: Created an Amazon EC2 Ubuntu Free Tier instance on AWS and configured it for cloud-based deployment. Installed and configured Docker on the EC2 instance to gain hands-on experience with containerization. Prepared Go (Golang) web services for deployment by creating Dockerfiles, building Docker images, and understanding container execution. Also attended the Industry Institution Interactive Meet (IIIM) organized by IIIT Kottayam in collaboration with BOAT, Chennai, under the Ministry of Education, Government of India, which provided insights into the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS 2.0), industry expectations, career opportunities, and the importance of industry–academia collaboration in enhancing practical skills and employability.

title: Day 24: IoT Fundamentals, MQTT & Tinkercad Circuit Simulations
description: Continued learning the fundamentals of IoT and Cloud Computing, with a focused session on the MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) protocol and its role in lightweight, publish-subscribe communication between IoT devices. Introduced to Tinkercad Circuits for virtual simulation-based learning, followed by hands-on implementation of three simulation tasks: LED Blinking using Arduino Uno, Temperature Sensor interfacing with Arduino, and PIR Motion Sensor simulation for motion detection. These activities enhanced understanding of basic sensor interfacing, Arduino programming, and virtual circuit simulation using Tinkercad.

title: Day 25: Docker Services Development & Practical Task Completion
description: Successfully completed Docker service programs and additional assigned practical tasks. Developed, tested, and ran containerized applications using Docker, strengthening understanding of container lifecycle management, Dockerfile authoring, image builds, and service orchestration. Gained significant hands-on experience in containerized application development workflows and improved practical development skills in cloud-native environments.

title: Day 26: Tinkercad IoT Circuit Design – Sensors & Actuators
description: Designed and simulated 10 sensor-based and 10 actuator-based circuits using Tinkercad and Arduino Uno. Activities included interfacing various IoT sensors and actuators, understanding their working principles, implementing Arduino programs, and verifying outputs through simulation. This hands-on practice strengthened practical knowledge of sensor integration, actuator control, circuit design, and Arduino programming for IoT applications.
